authors_text: Dylan Auty,Krystian Mikolajczyk
title: Monocular Depth Estimation Using Cues Inspired by Biological Vision Systems
title_zh: 使用受生物视觉系统启发的线索进行单目深度估计
comment: 7 pages, 2 figures. Accepted to International Conference on Pattern Recognition (ICPR) 2022. Code available at https://github.com/DylanAuty/MDE-biological-vision-systems
comments: 7 pages, 2 figures. Accepted to International Conference on Pattern Recognition (ICPR) 2022. Code available at https://github.com/DylanAuty/MDE-biological-vision-systems
abstract: Monocular depth estimation (MDE) aims to transform an RGB image of a scene into a pixelwise depth map from the same camera view. It is fundamentally ill-posed due to missing information: any single image can have been taken from many possible 3D scenes. Part of the MDE task is, therefore, to learn which visual cues in the image can be used for depth estimation, and how. With training data limited by cost of annotation or network capacity limited by computational power, this is challenging. In this work we demonstrate that explicitly injecting visual cue information into the model is beneficial for depth estimation. Following research into biological vision systems, we focus on semantic information and prior knowledge of object sizes and their relations, to emulate the biological cues of relative size, familiar size, and absolute size. We use state-of-the-art semantic and instance segmentation models to provide external information, and exploit language embeddings to encode relational information between classes. We also provide a prior on the average real-world size of objects. This external information overcomes the limitation in data availability, and ensures that the limited capacity of a given network is focused on known-helpful cues, therefore improving performance. We experimentally validate our hypothesis and evaluate the proposed model on the widely used NYUD2 indoor depth estimation benchmark. The results show improvements in depth prediction when the semantic information, size prior and instance size are explicitly provided along with the RGB images, and our method can be easily adapted to any depth estimation system.
summary: Monocular depth estimation (MDE) aims to transform an RGB image of a scene into a pixelwise depth map from the same camera view. It is fundamentally ill-posed due to missing information: any single image can have been taken from many possible 3D scenes. Part of the MDE task is, therefore, to learn which visual cues in the image can be used for depth estimation, and how. With training data limited by cost of annotation or network capacity limited by computational power, this is challenging. In this work we demonstrate that explicitly injecting visual cue information into the model is beneficial for depth estimation. Following research into biological vision systems, we focus on semantic information and prior knowledge of object sizes and their relations, to emulate the biological cues of relative size, familiar size, and absolute size. We use state-of-the-art semantic and instance segmentation models to provide external information, and exploit language embeddings to encode relational information between classes. We also provide a prior on the average real-world size of objects. This external information overcomes the limitation in data availability, and ensures that the limited capacity of a given network is focused on known-helpful cues, therefore improving performance. We experimentally validate our hypothesis and evaluate the proposed model on the widely used NYUD2 indoor depth estimation benchmark. The results show improvements in depth prediction when the semantic information, size prior and instance size are explicitly provided along with the RGB images, and our method can be easily adapted to any depth estimation system.
abstract_zh: 单目深度估计 (MDE) 旨在将场景的 RGB 图像转换为来自同一相机视图的像素深度图。由于缺少信息，它从根本上来说是不适定的：任何单个图像都可以从许多可能的 3D 场景中获取。因此，MDE 任务的一部分是了解图像中的哪些视觉线索可用于深度估计以及如何使用。由于训练数据受到注释成本的限制或网络容量受到计算能力的限制，这是具有挑战性的。在这项工作中，我们证明了将视觉提示信息显式注入模型有利于深度估计。在对生物视觉系统进行研究之后，我们专注于语义信息和物体尺寸及其关系的先验知识，以模拟相对尺寸、熟悉尺寸和绝对尺寸的生物线索。我们使用最先进的语义和实例分割模型来提供外部信息，并利用语言嵌入来编码类之间的关系信息。我们还提供了物体在现实世界中的平均大小的先验信息。这些外部信息克服了数据可用性的限制，并确保给定网络的有限容量集中在已知有用的线索上，从而提高性能。我们通过实验验证了我们的假设，并在广泛使用的 NYUD2 室内深度估计基准上评估了所提出的模型。结果表明，当语义信息、尺寸先验和实例尺寸与 RGB 图像一起明确提供时，深度预测会得到改进，并且我们的方法可以轻松适应任何深度估计系统。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种受生物视觉系统启发的单目深度估计方法，通过注入语义信息、物体尺寸先验和实例尺寸等视觉线索来提升深度预测性能。
AI.motivation: 单目深度估计因图像信息不足而面临挑战，现有方法受限于标注数据成本和网络计算能力，难以有效学习关键视觉线索。生物视觉系统中的相对尺寸、熟悉尺寸和绝对尺寸等线索为解决此问题提供了新思路。
AI.method: 结合语义分割模型提取语义信息，利用语言嵌入编码类别间关系，并引入物体平均真实尺寸先验。将这些外部信息与RGB图像联合输入网络，引导模型关注已知有效的深度估计线索。
AI.result: 在NYUD2室内深度估计基准测试中，当同时提供语义信息、尺寸先验和实例尺寸时，深度预测精度显著提升，且方法可适配任意深度估计系统。
AI.conclusion: 显式注入生物视觉启发的多模态线索能有效缓解数据不足问题，提升单目深度估计性能，为后续研究提供了可扩展的框架。

authors_text: Jessica Leu,Yebin Wang,Masayoshi Tomizuka,Stefano Di Cairano
title: Autonomous Vehicle Parking in Dynamic Environments: An Integrated System with Prediction and Motion Planning
title_zh: 动态环境中的自主车辆停车：具有预测和运动规划的集成系统
abstract: This paper presents an integrated motion planning system for autonomous vehicle (AV) parking in the presence of other moving vehicles. The proposed system includes 1) a hybrid environment predictor that predicts the motions of the surrounding vehicles and 2) a strategic motion planner that reacts to the predictions. The hybrid environment predictor performs short-term predictions via an extended Kalman filter and an adaptive observer. It also combines short-term predictions with a driver behavior cost-map to make long-term predictions. The strategic motion planner comprises 1) a model predictive control-based safety controller for trajectory tracking; 2) a search-based retreating planner for finding an evasion path in an emergency; 3) an optimization-based repairing planner for planning a new path when the original path is invalidated. Simulation validation demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed method in terms of initial planning, motion prediction, safe tracking, retreating in an emergency, and trajectory repairing.
summary: This paper presents an integrated motion planning system for autonomous vehicle (AV) parking in the presence of other moving vehicles. The proposed system includes 1) a hybrid environment predictor that predicts the motions of the surrounding vehicles and 2) a strategic motion planner that reacts to the predictions. The hybrid environment predictor performs short-term predictions via an extended Kalman filter and an adaptive observer. It also combines short-term predictions with a driver behavior cost-map to make long-term predictions. The strategic motion planner comprises 1) a model predictive control-based safety controller for trajectory tracking; 2) a search-based retreating planner for finding an evasion path in an emergency; 3) an optimization-based repairing planner for planning a new path when the original path is invalidated. Simulation validation demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed method in terms of initial planning, motion prediction, safe tracking, retreating in an emergency, and trajectory repairing.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种在存在其他移动车辆的情况下自动驾驶车辆（AV）停车的集成运动规划系统。所提出的系统包括 1) 一个混合环境预测器，用于预测周围车辆的运动；2) 一个对预测做出反应的策略运动规划器。混合环境预测器通过扩展卡尔曼滤波器和自适应观测器执行短期预测。它还将短期预测与驾驶员行为成本图相结合，以做出长期预测。策略运动规划器包括：1）基于模型预测控制的安全控制器，用于轨迹跟踪； 2）基于搜索的撤退规划器，用于在紧急情况下寻找逃生路径； 3）基于优化的修复规划器，用于在原始路径失效时规划新路径。仿真验证证明了该方法在初始规划、运动预测、安全跟踪、紧急撤退和轨迹修复等方面的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于自动驾驶车辆在动态环境中的停车系统，结合了环境预测与运动规划技术。
AI.motivation: 自动驾驶车辆在动态环境中停车时需应对周围移动车辆的不确定性，传统方法难以兼顾实时预测与安全路径规划。
AI.method: 系统包含两部分：1) 混合环境预测器，结合扩展卡尔曼滤波器和自适应观测器进行短期预测，并融合驾驶员行为代价图实现长期预测；2) 战略运动规划器，包含基于模型预测控制的安全控制器、基于搜索的紧急避让路径规划器及基于优化的路径修复规划器。
AI.result: 仿真验证表明，该方法在初始路径规划、运动预测、安全跟踪、紧急避让及轨迹修复方面均表现有效。
AI.conclusion: 该集成系统通过预测与规划的协同优化，提升了自动驾驶车辆在动态环境中的停车安全性与适应性。

search_text: Autonomous Vehicle Parking in Dynamic Environments: An Integrated System with Prediction and Motion Planning 动态环境中的自主车辆停车：具有预测和运动规划的集成系统 This paper presents an integrated motion planning system for autonomous vehicle (AV) parking in the presence of other moving vehicles. The proposed system includes 1) a hybrid environment predictor that predicts the motions of the surrounding vehicles and 2) a strategic motion planner that reacts to the predictions. The hybrid environment predictor performs short-term predictions via an extended Kalman filter and an adaptive observer. It also combines short-term predictions with a driver behavior cost-map to make long-term predictions. The strategic motion planner comprises 1) a model predictive control-based safety controller for trajectory tracking; 2) a search-based retreating planner for finding an evasion path in an emergency; 3) an optimization-based repairing planner for planning a new path when the original path is invalidated. Simulation validation demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed method in terms of initial planning, motion prediction, safe tracking, retreating in an emergency, and trajectory repairing. 本文提出了一种在存在其他移动车辆的情况下自动驾驶车辆（AV）停车的集成运动规划系统。所提出的系统包括 1) 一个混合环境预测器，用于预测周围车辆的运动；2) 一个对预测做出反应的策略运动规划器。混合环境预测器通过扩展卡尔曼滤波器和自适应观测器执行短期预测。它还将短期预测与驾驶员行为成本图相结合，以做出长期预测。策略运动规划器包括：1）基于模型预测控制的安全控制器，用于轨迹跟踪； 2）基于搜索的撤退规划器，用于在紧急情况下寻找逃生路径； 3）基于优化的修复规划器，用于在原始路径失效时规划新路径。仿真验证证明了该方法在初始规划、运动预测、安全跟踪、紧急撤退和轨迹修复等方面的有效性。 本文提出了一种用于自动驾驶车辆在动态环境中的停车系统，结合了环境预测与运动规划技术。 Jessica Leu,Yebin Wang,Masayoshi Tomizuka,Stefano Di Cairano cs.RO

authors_text: Chase Cockrell,Scott Christley,Gary An
title: Facilitating automated conversion of scientific knowledge into scientific simulation models with the Machine Assisted Generation, Calibration, and Comparison (MAGCC) Framework
title_zh: 利用机器辅助生成、校准和比较 (MAGCC) 框架促进科学知识自动转换为科学模拟模型
abstract: The Machine Assisted Generation, Comparison, and Calibration (MAGCC) framework provides machine assistance and automation of recurrent crucial steps and processes in the development, implementation, testing, and use of scientific simulation models. MAGCC bridges systems for knowledge extraction via natural language processing or extracted from existing mathematical models and provides a comprehensive workflow encompassing the composition of scientific models and artificial intelligence (AI) assisted code generation. MAGCC accomplishes this through: 1) the development of a comprehensively expressive formal knowledge representation knowledgebase, the Structured Scientific Knowledge Representation (SSKR) that encompasses all the types of information needed to make any simulation model, 2) the use of an artificially intelligent logic reasoning system, the Computational Modeling Assistant (CMA), that takes information from the SSKR and generates, in a traceable fashion, model specifications across a range of simulation modeling methods, and 3) the use of the CMA to generate executable code for a simulation model from those model specifications. The MAGCC framework can be customized any scientific domain, and future work will integrate newly developed code-generating AI systems.
summary: The Machine Assisted Generation, Comparison, and Calibration (MAGCC) framework provides machine assistance and automation of recurrent crucial steps and processes in the development, implementation, testing, and use of scientific simulation models. MAGCC bridges systems for knowledge extraction via natural language processing or extracted from existing mathematical models and provides a comprehensive workflow encompassing the composition of scientific models and artificial intelligence (AI) assisted code generation. MAGCC accomplishes this through: 1) the development of a comprehensively expressive formal knowledge representation knowledgebase, the Structured Scientific Knowledge Representation (SSKR) that encompasses all the types of information needed to make any simulation model, 2) the use of an artificially intelligent logic reasoning system, the Computational Modeling Assistant (CMA), that takes information from the SSKR and generates, in a traceable fashion, model specifications across a range of simulation modeling methods, and 3) the use of the CMA to generate executable code for a simulation model from those model specifications. The MAGCC framework can be customized any scientific domain, and future work will integrate newly developed code-generating AI systems.
abstract_zh: 机器辅助生成、比较和校准 (MAGCC) 框架为科学仿真模型的开发、实施、测试和使用中的重复关键步骤和流程提供机器辅助和自动化。 MAGCC 连接了通过自然语言处理或从现有数学模型中提取知识的系统，并提供了涵盖科学模型组成和人工智能 (AI) 辅助代码生成的全面工作流程。 MAGCC 通过以下方式实现这一目标：1) 开发一个全面表达的形式知识表示知识库，即结构化科学知识表示 (SSKR)，其中包含制作任何仿真模型所需的所有类型的信息，2) 使用人工智能逻辑推理系统，即计算建模助手 (CMA)，它从 SSKR 获取信息并以可追溯的方式生成跨一系列仿真建模方法的模型规范，以及 3) 使用CMA 根据这些模型规范生成仿真模型的可执行代码。 MAGCC框架可以定制任何科学领域，未来的工作将集成新开发的代码生成人工智能系统。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MAGCC框架，通过机器辅助生成、校准和比较，实现科学知识向模拟模型的自动化转换。
AI.motivation: 科学模拟模型的开发过程复杂且耗时，需整合知识提取与代码生成，现有方法效率低且易出错。
AI.method: 构建结构化科学知识表示库（SSKR）存储多类型信息，利用计算建模助手（CMA）从SSKR生成模型规范并自动编写可执行代码。
AI.result: MAGCC框架实现了跨领域科学模型的自动化生成与校准，支持定制化扩展，并为未来集成新型AI代码生成系统奠定基础。
AI.conclusion: MAGCC通过知识表示与AI推理的结合，显著提升科学模拟模型开发效率与准确性，具有广泛的应用前景。

authors_text: Milind Naphade,Shuo Wang,David C. Anastasiu,Zheng Tang,Ming-Ching Chang,Yue Yao,Liang Zheng,Mohammed Shaiqur Rahman,Archana Venkatachalapathy,Anuj Sharma,Qi Feng,Vitaly Ablavsky,Stan Sclaroff,Pranamesh Chakraborty,Alice Li,Shangru Li,Rama Chellappa
title: The 6th AI City Challenge
title_zh: 第六届人工智能城市挑战赛
comment: Summary of the 6th AI City Challenge Workshop in conjunction with CVPR 2022. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2104.12233
comments: Summary of the 6th AI City Challenge Workshop in conjunction with CVPR 2022. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2104.12233
abstract: The 6th edition of the AI City Challenge specifically focuses on problems in two domains where there is tremendous unlocked potential at the intersection of computer vision and artificial intelligence: Intelligent Traffic Systems (ITS), and brick and mortar retail businesses. The four challenge tracks of the 2022 AI City Challenge received participation requests from 254 teams across 27 countries. Track 1 addressed city-scale multi-target multi-camera (MTMC) vehicle tracking. Track 2 addressed natural-language-based vehicle track retrieval. Track 3 was a brand new track for naturalistic driving analysis, where the data were captured by several cameras mounted inside the vehicle focusing on driver safety, and the task was to classify driver actions. Track 4 was another new track aiming to achieve retail store automated checkout using only a single view camera. We released two leader boards for submissions based on different methods, including a public leader board for the contest, where no use of external data is allowed, and a general leader board for all submitted results. The top performance of participating teams established strong baselines and even outperformed the state-of-the-art in the proposed challenge tracks.
summary: The 6th edition of the AI City Challenge specifically focuses on problems in two domains where there is tremendous unlocked potential at the intersection of computer vision and artificial intelligence: Intelligent Traffic Systems (ITS), and brick and mortar retail businesses. The four challenge tracks of the 2022 AI City Challenge received participation requests from 254 teams across 27 countries. Track 1 addressed city-scale multi-target multi-camera (MTMC) vehicle tracking. Track 2 addressed natural-language-based vehicle track retrieval. Track 3 was a brand new track for naturalistic driving analysis, where the data were captured by several cameras mounted inside the vehicle focusing on driver safety, and the task was to classify driver actions. Track 4 was another new track aiming to achieve retail store automated checkout using only a single view camera. We released two leader boards for submissions based on different methods, including a public leader board for the contest, where no use of external data is allowed, and a general leader board for all submitted results. The top performance of participating teams established strong baselines and even outperformed the state-of-the-art in the proposed challenge tracks.
abstract_zh: 第六版人工智能城市挑战赛特别关注计算机视觉和人工智能交叉领域具有巨大潜力的两个领域的问题：智能交通系统（ITS）和实体零售业务。 2022 年人工智能城市挑战赛的四个挑战赛道收到了来自 27 个国家的 254 个团队的参赛请求。第 1 轨道解决了城市规模的多目标多摄像头 (MTMC) 车辆跟踪。 Track 2 解决基于自然语言的车辆轨迹检索。 Track 3是一条全新的自然驾驶分析赛道，数据由安装在车内的多个摄像头捕获，重点关注驾驶员安全，任务是对驾驶员行为进行分类。 Track 4 是另一条新赛道，旨在仅使用单视摄像头实现零售店自动结账。我们根据不同的方式发布了两个提交排行榜，其中包括不允许使用外部数据的竞赛公共排行榜，以及所有提交结果的通用排行榜。参赛团队的最佳表现奠定了坚实的基础，甚至在拟议的挑战赛中超越了最先进的水平。

AI.tldr: 该论文介绍了第六届AI City挑战赛，聚焦智能交通系统和实体零售领域，包含四个赛道：城市级多目标多摄像头车辆追踪、自然语言车辆轨迹检索、自然驾驶行为分析及单视角零售自动结账。
AI.motivation: 旨在通过计算机视觉与人工智能技术挖掘交通与零售场景的潜力，推动相关技术发展并解决实际问题。
AI.method: 设置四个挑战赛道，采用两种榜单（公开榜与总榜）评估参赛队伍，限制外部数据使用，鼓励算法创新。
AI.result: 参赛团队在多个赛道上建立了强基线，部分表现超越现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: 挑战赛有效促进了AI与计算机视觉在交通和零售领域的应用，为未来研究提供了重要参考。

search_text: The 6th AI City Challenge 第六届人工智能城市挑战赛 The 6th edition of the AI City Challenge specifically focuses on problems in two domains where there is tremendous unlocked potential at the intersection of computer vision and artificial intelligence: Intelligent Traffic Systems (ITS), and brick and mortar retail businesses. The four challenge tracks of the 2022 AI City Challenge received participation requests from 254 teams across 27 countries. Track 1 addressed city-scale multi-target multi-camera (MTMC) vehicle tracking. Track 2 addressed natural-language-based vehicle track retrieval. Track 3 was a brand new track for naturalistic driving analysis, where the data were captured by several cameras mounted inside the vehicle focusing on driver safety, and the task was to classify driver actions. Track 4 was another new track aiming to achieve retail store automated checkout using only a single view camera. We released two leader boards for submissions based on different methods, including a public leader board for the contest, where no use of external data is allowed, and a general leader board for all submitted results. The top performance of participating teams established strong baselines and even outperformed the state-of-the-art in the proposed challenge tracks. 第六版人工智能城市挑战赛特别关注计算机视觉和人工智能交叉领域具有巨大潜力的两个领域的问题：智能交通系统（ITS）和实体零售业务。 2022 年人工智能城市挑战赛的四个挑战赛道收到了来自 27 个国家的 254 个团队的参赛请求。第 1 轨道解决了城市规模的多目标多摄像头 (MTMC) 车辆跟踪。 Track 2 解决基于自然语言的车辆轨迹检索。 Track 3是一条全新的自然驾驶分析赛道，数据由安装在车内的多个摄像头捕获，重点关注驾驶员安全，任务是对驾驶员行为进行分类。 Track 4 是另一条新赛道，旨在仅使用单视摄像头实现零售店自动结账。我们根据不同的方式发布了两个提交排行榜，其中包括不允许使用外部数据的竞赛公共排行榜，以及所有提交结果的通用排行榜。参赛团队的最佳表现奠定了坚实的基础，甚至在拟议的挑战赛中超越了最先进的水平。 该论文介绍了第六届AI City挑战赛，聚焦智能交通系统和实体零售领域，包含四个赛道：城市级多目标多摄像头车辆追踪、自然语言车辆轨迹检索、自然驾驶行为分析及单视角零售自动结账。 Milind Naphade,Shuo Wang,David C. Anastasiu,Zheng Tang,Ming-Ching Chang,Yue Yao,Liang Zheng,Mohammed Shaiqur Rahman,Archana Venkatachalapathy,Anuj Sharma,Qi Feng,Vitaly Ablavsky,Stan Sclaroff,Pranamesh Chakraborty,Alice Li,Shangru Li,Rama Chellappa cs.CV

authors_text: Dian Chen,Dequan Wang,Trevor Darrell,Sayna Ebrahimi
title: Contrastive Test-Time Adaptation
title_zh: 对比测试时间适应
abstract: Test-time adaptation is a special setting of unsupervised domain adaptation where a trained model on the source domain has to adapt to the target domain without accessing source data. We propose a novel way to leverage self-supervised contrastive learning to facilitate target feature learning, along with an online pseudo labeling scheme with refinement that significantly denoises pseudo labels. The contrastive learning task is applied jointly with pseudo labeling, contrasting positive and negative pairs constructed similarly as MoCo but with source-initialized encoder, and excluding same-class negative pairs indicated by pseudo labels. Meanwhile, we produce pseudo labels online and refine them via soft voting among their nearest neighbors in the target feature space, enabled by maintaining a memory queue. Our method, AdaContrast, achieves state-of-the-art performance on major benchmarks while having several desirable properties compared to existing works, including memory efficiency, insensitivity to hyper-parameters, and better model calibration. Project page: sites.google.com/view/adacontrast.
summary: Test-time adaptation is a special setting of unsupervised domain adaptation where a trained model on the source domain has to adapt to the target domain without accessing source data. We propose a novel way to leverage self-supervised contrastive learning to facilitate target feature learning, along with an online pseudo labeling scheme with refinement that significantly denoises pseudo labels. The contrastive learning task is applied jointly with pseudo labeling, contrasting positive and negative pairs constructed similarly as MoCo but with source-initialized encoder, and excluding same-class negative pairs indicated by pseudo labels. Meanwhile, we produce pseudo labels online and refine them via soft voting among their nearest neighbors in the target feature space, enabled by maintaining a memory queue. Our method, AdaContrast, achieves state-of-the-art performance on major benchmarks while having several desirable properties compared to existing works, including memory efficiency, insensitivity to hyper-parameters, and better model calibration. Project page: sites.google.com/view/adacontrast.
abstract_zh: 测试时适应是无监督域适应的特殊设置，其中源域上经过训练的模型必须适应目标域而不访问源数据。我们提出了一种利用自监督对比学习来促进目标特征学习的新颖方法，以及一种经过细化的在线伪标签方案，可显着去噪伪标签。对比学习任务与伪标签联合应用，对比与 MoCo 类似但使用源初始化编码器构造的正负对，并排除伪标签指示的同类负对。同时，我们在线生成伪标签，并通过目标特征空间中最近邻居之间的软投票来细化它们，这通过维护内存队列来实现。我们的方法 AdaContrast 在主要基准测试中实现了最先进的性能，同时与现有方法相比具有几个理想的特性，包括内存效率、对超参数不敏感以及更好的模型校准。项目页面：sites.google.com/view/adacontrast。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于对比学习和伪标签优化的测试时域适应方法AdaContrast，用于在无源域数据的情况下提升目标域模型性能。
AI.motivation: 现有测试时域适应方法存在伪标签噪声大、对超参数敏感等问题，需设计更鲁棒且高效的适应框架。
AI.method: 结合自监督对比学习与在线伪标签优化，通过记忆队列实现目标特征空间的软投票去噪，并利用源域初始化编码器构造正负样本对进行对比学习。
AI.result: 在主流基准数据集上达到SOTA性能，具有内存效率高、超参数不敏感和模型校准更优等特性。
AI.conclusion: AdaContrast通过联合优化对比学习与伪标签精炼，为测试时域适应提供了有效且实用的解决方案。

authors_text: Anoop K.,Manjary P. Gangan,Deepak P.,Lajish V. L
title: Towards an Enhanced Understanding of Bias in Pre-trained Neural Language Models: A Survey with Special Emphasis on Affective Bias
title_zh: 加强对预训练神经语言模型中偏见的理解：一项特别强调情感偏见的调查
abstract: The remarkable progress in Natural Language Processing (NLP) brought about by deep learning, particularly with the recent advent of large pre-trained neural language models, is brought into scrutiny as several studies began to discuss and report potential biases in NLP applications. Bias in NLP is found to originate from latent historical biases encoded by humans into textual data which gets perpetuated or even amplified by NLP algorithm. We present a survey to comprehend bias in large pre-trained language models, analyze the stages at which they occur in these models, and various ways in which these biases could be quantified and mitigated. Considering wide applicability of textual affective computing based downstream tasks in real-world systems such as business, healthcare, education, etc., we give a special emphasis on investigating bias in the context of affect (emotion) i.e., Affective Bias, in large pre-trained language models. We present a summary of various bias evaluation corpora that help to aid future research and discuss challenges in the research on bias in pre-trained language models. We believe that our attempt to draw a comprehensive view of bias in pre-trained language models, and especially the exploration of affective bias will be highly beneficial to researchers interested in this evolving field.
summary: The remarkable progress in Natural Language Processing (NLP) brought about by deep learning, particularly with the recent advent of large pre-trained neural language models, is brought into scrutiny as several studies began to discuss and report potential biases in NLP applications. Bias in NLP is found to originate from latent historical biases encoded by humans into textual data which gets perpetuated or even amplified by NLP algorithm. We present a survey to comprehend bias in large pre-trained language models, analyze the stages at which they occur in these models, and various ways in which these biases could be quantified and mitigated. Considering wide applicability of textual affective computing based downstream tasks in real-world systems such as business, healthcare, education, etc., we give a special emphasis on investigating bias in the context of affect (emotion) i.e., Affective Bias, in large pre-trained language models. We present a summary of various bias evaluation corpora that help to aid future research and discuss challenges in the research on bias in pre-trained language models. We believe that our attempt to draw a comprehensive view of bias in pre-trained language models, and especially the exploration of affective bias will be highly beneficial to researchers interested in this evolving field.
abstract_zh: 深度学习给自然语言处理 (NLP) 带来的显着进展，特别是最近大型预训练神经语言模型的出现，随着一些研究开始讨论和报告 NLP 应用中的潜在偏差，受到密切关注。人们发现，NLP 中的偏差源自人类编码为文本数据的潜在历史偏差，这些偏差被 NLP 算法永久化甚至放大。我们提出了一项调查，以了解大型预训练语言模型中的偏差，分析它们在这些模型中出现的阶段，以及量化和减轻这些偏差的各种方法。考虑到基于文本情感计算的下游任务在商业、医疗、教育等现实世界系统中的广泛适用性，我们特别强调在大型预训练语言模型中调查情感（情感）背景下的偏见，即情感偏见。我们总结了各种偏差评估语料库，有助于帮助未来的研究，并讨论预训练语言模型偏差研究中的挑战。我们相信，我们尝试对预训练语言模型中的偏见进行全面的了解，特别是对情感偏见的探索，对于对这个不断发展的领域感兴趣的研究人员将非常有益。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了预训练神经语言模型中的偏见问题，重点分析情感偏见的来源、量化方法及缓解策略，并总结相关评估语料库与研究挑战。
AI.motivation: 深度学习推动的NLP应用中存在潜在偏见，历史数据中的隐性偏见可能被算法放大，需系统理解并解决这一问题，尤其关注情感计算场景下的影响。
AI.method: 通过文献综述分析偏见产生的阶段，总结量化与缓解方法，整理现有偏见评估语料库，并讨论研究中的技术与实践挑战。
AI.result: 总结了偏见评估数据集，分析了不同阶段偏见表现形式，指出当前研究在数据多样性、评估标准及缓解方法有效性上的不足。
AI.conclusion: 系统梳理预训练模型偏见问题对研究具有指导意义，未来需加强跨领域数据研究、动态评估体系构建及可解释性技术开发。

conclusion: 系统梳理预训练模型偏见问题对研究具有指导意义，未来需加强跨领域数据研究、动态评估体系构建及可解释性技术开发。
search_text: Towards an Enhanced Understanding of Bias in Pre-trained Neural Language Models: A Survey with Special Emphasis on Affective Bias 加强对预训练神经语言模型中偏见的理解：一项特别强调情感偏见的调查 The remarkable progress in Natural Language Processing (NLP) brought about by deep learning, particularly with the recent advent of large pre-trained neural language models, is brought into scrutiny as several studies began to discuss and report potential biases in NLP applications. Bias in NLP is found to originate from latent historical biases encoded by humans into textual data which gets perpetuated or even amplified by NLP algorithm. We present a survey to comprehend bias in large pre-trained language models, analyze the stages at which they occur in these models, and various ways in which these biases could be quantified and mitigated. Considering wide applicability of textual affective computing based downstream tasks in real-world systems such as business, healthcare, education, etc., we give a special emphasis on investigating bias in the context of affect (emotion) i.e., Affective Bias, in large pre-trained language models. We present a summary of various bias evaluation corpora that help to aid future research and discuss challenges in the research on bias in pre-trained language models. We believe that our attempt to draw a comprehensive view of bias in pre-trained language models, and especially the exploration of affective bias will be highly beneficial to researchers interested in this evolving field. 深度学习给自然语言处理 (NLP) 带来的显着进展，特别是最近大型预训练神经语言模型的出现，随着一些研究开始讨论和报告 NLP 应用中的潜在偏差，受到密切关注。人们发现，NLP 中的偏差源自人类编码为文本数据的潜在历史偏差，这些偏差被 NLP 算法永久化甚至放大。我们提出了一项调查，以了解大型预训练语言模型中的偏差，分析它们在这些模型中出现的阶段，以及量化和减轻这些偏差的各种方法。考虑到基于文本情感计算的下游任务在商业、医疗、教育等现实世界系统中的广泛适用性，我们特别强调在大型预训练语言模型中调查情感（情感）背景下的偏见，即情感偏见。我们总结了各种偏差评估语料库，有助于帮助未来的研究，并讨论预训练语言模型偏差研究中的挑战。我们相信，我们尝试对预训练语言模型中的偏见进行全面的了解，特别是对情感偏见的探索，对于对这个不断发展的领域感兴趣的研究人员将非常有益。 本文综述了预训练神经语言模型中的偏见问题，重点分析情感偏见的来源、量化方法及缓解策略，并总结相关评估语料库与研究挑战。 Anoop K.,Manjary P. Gangan,Deepak P.,Lajish V. L cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Evana Gizzi,Lakshmi Nair,Sonia Chernova,Jivko Sinapov
title: Creative Problem Solving in Artificially Intelligent Agents: A Survey and Framework
title_zh: 人工智能代理的创造性问题解决：调查和框架
comment: 46 pages (including appendix), 17 figures, under submission at Journal of Artificial Intelligence Research (JAIR)
comments: 46 pages (including appendix), 17 figures, under submission at Journal of Artificial Intelligence Research (JAIR)
abstract: Creative Problem Solving (CPS) is a sub-area within Artificial Intelligence (AI) that focuses on methods for solving off-nominal, or anomalous problems in autonomous systems. Despite many advancements in planning and learning, resolving novel problems or adapting existing knowledge to a new context, especially in cases where the environment may change in unpredictable ways post deployment, remains a limiting factor in the safe and useful integration of intelligent systems. The emergence of increasingly autonomous systems dictates the necessity for AI agents to deal with environmental uncertainty through creativity. To stimulate further research in CPS, we present a definition and a framework of CPS, which we adopt to categorize existing AI methods in this field. Our framework consists of four main components of a CPS problem, namely, 1) problem formulation, 2) knowledge representation, 3) method of knowledge manipulation, and 4) method of evaluation. We conclude our survey with open research questions, and suggested directions for the future.
summary: Creative Problem Solving (CPS) is a sub-area within Artificial Intelligence (AI) that focuses on methods for solving off-nominal, or anomalous problems in autonomous systems. Despite many advancements in planning and learning, resolving novel problems or adapting existing knowledge to a new context, especially in cases where the environment may change in unpredictable ways post deployment, remains a limiting factor in the safe and useful integration of intelligent systems. The emergence of increasingly autonomous systems dictates the necessity for AI agents to deal with environmental uncertainty through creativity. To stimulate further research in CPS, we present a definition and a framework of CPS, which we adopt to categorize existing AI methods in this field. Our framework consists of four main components of a CPS problem, namely, 1) problem formulation, 2) knowledge representation, 3) method of knowledge manipulation, and 4) method of evaluation. We conclude our survey with open research questions, and suggested directions for the future.
abstract_zh: 创造性问题解决 (CPS) 是人工智能 (AI) 中的一个子领域，专注于解决自主系统中的非正常或异常问题的方法。尽管在规划和学习方面取得了许多进步，但解决新问题或使现有知识适应新环境，尤其是在部署后环境可能以不可预测的方式发生变化的情况下，仍然是智能系统安全和有用集成的限制因素。日益自治的系统的出现决定了人工智能代理必须通过创造力来应对环境的不确定性。为了促进 CPS 的进一步研究，我们提出了 CPS 的定义和框架，我们采用它来对该领域现有的 AI 方法进行分类。我们的框架由 CPS 问题的四个主要组成部分组成，即 1）问题表述、2）知识表示、3）知识操作方法和 4）评估方法。我们以开放的研究问题结束我们的调查，并提出未来的方向。

AI.tldr: 综述现有AI方法并提出一个包含问题定义、知识表示、知识操作方法和评估方法的CPS框架
AI.motivation: 自主系统需应对环境不确定性，现有方法在解决新问题和适应环境变化方面存在不足，需推动CPS研究
AI.method: 提出包含问题定义、知识表示、知识操作方法和评估方法的四部分框架，用于分类现有CPS方法
AI.result: 通过框架分类现有方法，识别研究空白，并提出未来研究方向
AI.conclusion: CPS对自主系统处理环境不确定性至关重要，需进一步研究以提升适应性和安全性

authors_text: Karan Taneja,Harshvardhan Sikka,Ashok Goel
title: A Framework for Interactive Knowledge-Aided Machine Teaching
title_zh: 交互式知识辅助机器教学框架
abstract: Machine Teaching (MT) is an interactive process where humans train a machine learning model by playing the role of a teacher. The process of designing an MT system involves decisions that can impact both efficiency of human teachers and performance of machine learners. Previous research has proposed and evaluated specific MT systems but there is limited discussion on a general framework for designing them. We propose a framework for designing MT systems and also detail a system for the text classification problem as a specific instance. Our framework focuses on three components i.e. teaching interface, machine learner, and knowledge base; and their relations describe how each component can benefit the others. Our preliminary experiments show how MT systems can reduce both human teaching time and machine learner error rate.
summary: Machine Teaching (MT) is an interactive process where humans train a machine learning model by playing the role of a teacher. The process of designing an MT system involves decisions that can impact both efficiency of human teachers and performance of machine learners. Previous research has proposed and evaluated specific MT systems but there is limited discussion on a general framework for designing them. We propose a framework for designing MT systems and also detail a system for the text classification problem as a specific instance. Our framework focuses on three components i.e. teaching interface, machine learner, and knowledge base; and their relations describe how each component can benefit the others. Our preliminary experiments show how MT systems can reduce both human teaching time and machine learner error rate.
abstract_zh: 机器教学（MT）是一个交互式过程，人类通过扮演老师的角色来训练机器学习模型。设计机器翻译系统的过程涉及的决策可能会影响人类教师的效率和机器学习者的表现。先前的研究已经提出并评估了特定的机器翻译系统，但对设计它们的通用框架的讨论却很有限。我们提出了一个设计机器翻译系统的框架，并作为一个具体实例详细介绍了一个用于文本分类问题的系统。我们的框架侧重于三个组成部分，即教学界面、机器学习器和知识库；它们之间的关系描述了每个组成部分如何使其他组成部分受益。我们的初步实验展示了机器翻译系统如何减少人类教学时间和机器学习者的错误率。

AI.tldr: 提出一个交互式知识辅助机器教学的通用框架，并以文本分类为例进行具体实现。
AI.motivation: 现有机器教学系统设计缺乏通用框架，难以兼顾教师效率与模型性能，需构建系统化设计方法。
AI.method: 构建包含教学接口、机器学习器和知识库的三元框架，明确各组件协同机制，并在文本分类任务中实例化该框架。
AI.result: 实验表明，该框架能有效降低人类教师教学时间与机器学习模型错误率。
AI.conclusion: 提出的框架为交互式机器教学系统设计提供了通用方法论，验证了知识辅助机制的有效性，对后续研究具有指导意义。

authors_text: Chengyuan Xu,Boning Dong,Noah Stier,Curtis McCully,D. Andrew Howell,Pradeep Sen,Tobias Höllerer
title: Interactive Segmentation and Visualization for Tiny Objects in Multi-megapixel Images
title_zh: 百万像素图像中微小物体的交互式分割和可视化
comment: 6 pages, 4 figures. Accepted by CVPR 2022 Demo Program
comments: 6 pages, 4 figures. Accepted by CVPR 2022 Demo Program
abstract: We introduce an interactive image segmentation and visualization framework for identifying, inspecting, and editing tiny objects (just a few pixels wide) in large multi-megapixel high-dynamic-range (HDR) images. Detecting cosmic rays (CRs) in astronomical observations is a cumbersome workflow that requires multiple tools, so we developed an interactive toolkit that unifies model inference, HDR image visualization, segmentation mask inspection and editing into a single graphical user interface. The feature set, initially designed for astronomical data, makes this work a useful research-supporting tool for human-in-the-loop tiny-object segmentation in scientific areas like biomedicine, materials science, remote sensing, etc., as well as computer vision. Our interface features mouse-controlled, synchronized, dual-window visualization of the image and the segmentation mask, a critical feature for locating tiny objects in multi-megapixel images. The browser-based tool can be readily hosted on the web to provide multi-user access and GPU acceleration for any device. The toolkit can also be used as a high-precision annotation tool, or adapted as the frontend for an interactive machine learning framework. Our open-source dataset, CR detection model, and visualization toolkit are available at https://github.com/cy-xu/cosmic-conn.
summary: We introduce an interactive image segmentation and visualization framework for identifying, inspecting, and editing tiny objects (just a few pixels wide) in large multi-megapixel high-dynamic-range (HDR) images. Detecting cosmic rays (CRs) in astronomical observations is a cumbersome workflow that requires multiple tools, so we developed an interactive toolkit that unifies model inference, HDR image visualization, segmentation mask inspection and editing into a single graphical user interface. The feature set, initially designed for astronomical data, makes this work a useful research-supporting tool for human-in-the-loop tiny-object segmentation in scientific areas like biomedicine, materials science, remote sensing, etc., as well as computer vision. Our interface features mouse-controlled, synchronized, dual-window visualization of the image and the segmentation mask, a critical feature for locating tiny objects in multi-megapixel images. The browser-based tool can be readily hosted on the web to provide multi-user access and GPU acceleration for any device. The toolkit can also be used as a high-precision annotation tool, or adapted as the frontend for an interactive machine learning framework. Our open-source dataset, CR detection model, and visualization toolkit are available at https://github.com/cy-xu/cosmic-conn.
abstract_zh: 我们引入了一种交互式图像分割和可视化框架，用于识别、检查和编辑大型百万像素高动态范围 (HDR) 图像中的微小对象（只有几个像素宽）。在天文观测中检测宇宙射线 (CR) 是一个繁琐的工作流程，需要多种工具，因此我们开发了一个交互式工具包，将模型推理、HDR 图像可视化、分割掩模检查和编辑统一到单个图形用户界面中。该功能集最初是为天文数据设计的，使这项工作成为生物医学、材料科学、遥感等科学领域以及计算机视觉等人机循环微小物体分割的有用研究支持工具。我们的界面具有鼠标控制、同步、双窗口可视化图像和分割蒙版，这是在数百万像素图像中定位微小物体的关键功能。基于浏览器的工具可以轻松托管在网络上，为任何设备提供多用户访问和 GPU 加速。该工具包还可以用作高精度注释工具，或用作交互式机器学习框架的前端。我们的开源数据集、CR 检测模型和可视化工具包可在 https://github.com/cy-xu/cosmic-conn 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种交互式图像分割与可视化框架，用于在多兆像素高动态范围（HDR）图像中识别、检查和编辑微小物体（如宇宙射线）。
AI.motivation: 现有宇宙射线检测工作流程需依赖多个工具，效率低下，缺乏统一的交互式处理界面，难以满足科学领域对微小物体分割的需求。
AI.method: 开发基于浏览器的交互式工具，整合模型推理、HDR图像可视化、分割掩码检查与编辑功能，采用双窗口同步可视化技术，并支持多用户访问与GPU加速。
AI.result: 开源了宇宙射线检测数据集、模型及可视化工具（GitHub链接），验证了工具在天文学、生物医学等领域的通用性，并可作为高精度标注工具或机器学习前端框架。
AI.conclusion: 该工具通过统一交互界面解决了多工具协作的痛点，为科学领域微小物体分割提供了高效解决方案，并具备跨学科应用潜力。

search_text: Interactive Segmentation and Visualization for Tiny Objects in Multi-megapixel Images 百万像素图像中微小物体的交互式分割和可视化 We introduce an interactive image segmentation and visualization framework for identifying, inspecting, and editing tiny objects (just a few pixels wide) in large multi-megapixel high-dynamic-range (HDR) images. Detecting cosmic rays (CRs) in astronomical observations is a cumbersome workflow that requires multiple tools, so we developed an interactive toolkit that unifies model inference, HDR image visualization, segmentation mask inspection and editing into a single graphical user interface. The feature set, initially designed for astronomical data, makes this work a useful research-supporting tool for human-in-the-loop tiny-object segmentation in scientific areas like biomedicine, materials science, remote sensing, etc., as well as computer vision. Our interface features mouse-controlled, synchronized, dual-window visualization of the image and the segmentation mask, a critical feature for locating tiny objects in multi-megapixel images. The browser-based tool can be readily hosted on the web to provide multi-user access and GPU acceleration for any device. The toolkit can also be used as a high-precision annotation tool, or adapted as the frontend for an interactive machine learning framework. Our open-source dataset, CR detection model, and visualization toolkit are available at https://github.com/cy-xu/cosmic-conn. 我们引入了一种交互式图像分割和可视化框架，用于识别、检查和编辑大型百万像素高动态范围 (HDR) 图像中的微小对象（只有几个像素宽）。在天文观测中检测宇宙射线 (CR) 是一个繁琐的工作流程，需要多种工具，因此我们开发了一个交互式工具包，将模型推理、HDR 图像可视化、分割掩模检查和编辑统一到单个图形用户界面中。该功能集最初是为天文数据设计的，使这项工作成为生物医学、材料科学、遥感等科学领域以及计算机视觉等人机循环微小物体分割的有用研究支持工具。我们的界面具有鼠标控制、同步、双窗口可视化图像和分割蒙版，这是在数百万像素图像中定位微小物体的关键功能。基于浏览器的工具可以轻松托管在网络上，为任何设备提供多用户访问和 GPU 加速。该工具包还可以用作高精度注释工具，或用作交互式机器学习框架的前端。我们的开源数据集、CR 检测模型和可视化工具包可在 https://github.com/cy-xu/cosmic-conn 上获取。 本文提出一种交互式图像分割与可视化框架，用于在多兆像素高动态范围（HDR）图像中识别、检查和编辑微小物体（如宇宙射线）。 Chengyuan Xu,Boning Dong,Noah Stier,Curtis McCully,D. Andrew Howell,Pradeep Sen,Tobias Höllerer cs.CV,astro-ph.IM,cs.HC

authors_text: Magdalene Ng,Kovila P. L. Coopamootoo,Tasos Spiliotopoulos,Dave Horsfall,Mhairi Aitken,Ehsan Toreini,Karen Elliott,Aad van Moorsel
title: In Private, Secure, Conversational FinBots We Trust
title_zh: 我们信任的私密、安全、对话式 FinBots
comment: Proceedings of the CHI 2021 Workshop on Let's Talk About CUIs: Putting Conversational User Interface Design into Practice, May 8, 2021 in Yokohama, Japan
comments: Proceedings of the CHI 2021 Workshop on Let's Talk About CUIs: Putting Conversational User Interface Design into Practice, May 8, 2021 in Yokohama, Japan
abstract: In the past decade, the financial industry has experienced a technology revolution. While we witness a rapid introduction of conversational bots for financial services, there is a lack of understanding of conversational user interfaces (CUI) features in this domain. The finance industry also deals with highly sensitive information and monetary transactions, presenting a challenge for developers and financial providers. Through a study on how to design text-based conversational financial interfaces with N=410 participants, we outline user requirements of trustworthy CUI design for financial bots. We posit that, in the context of Finance, bot privacy and security assurances outweigh conversational capability and postulate implications of these findings. This work acts as a resource on how to design trustworthy FinBots and demonstrates how automated financial advisors can be transformed into trusted everyday devices, capable of supporting users' daily financial activities.

While we witness a rapid introduction of conversational bots for financial services, there is a lack of understanding of conversational user interfaces (CUI) features in this domain. The finance industry also deals with highly sensitive information and monetary transactions, presenting a challenge for developers and financial providers. Through a study on how to design text-based conversational financial interfaces with N=410 participants, we outline user requirements of trustworthy CUI design for financial bots. We posit that, in the context of Finance, bot privacy and security assurances outweigh conversational capability and postulate implications of these findings. This work acts as a resource on how to design trustworthy FinBots and demonstrates how automated financial advisors can be transformed into trusted everyday devices, capable of supporting users' daily financial activities.
abstract_zh: 过去十年，金融行业经历了一场技术革命。虽然我们见证了金融服务领域对话机器人的快速引入，但人们对该领域的对话用户界面 (CUI) 功能缺乏了解。金融行业还处理高度敏感的信息和货币交易，这给开发商和金融提供商带来了挑战。通过研究如何设计 N=410 名参与者的基于文本的对话式金融界面，我们概述了金融机器人值得信赖的 CUI 设计的用户需求。我们认为，在金融领域，机器人隐私和安全保证比对话能力更重要，并假设这些发现的影响。这项工作作为如何设计值得信赖的 FinBot 的资源，并展示了如何将自动化财务顾问转变为值得信赖的日常设备，能够支持用户的日常财务活动。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在探讨如何设计可信的金融聊天机器人（FinBots），重点分析隐私和安全功能在金融领域对话式用户界面（CUI）中的重要性。
AI.motivation: 金融行业面临敏感信息和交易的安全挑战，但现有研究缺乏对金融场景下对话式界面（CUI）核心需求的系统性分析，尤其是隐私与安全的优先级问题。
AI.method: 通过一项包含410名参与者的实证研究，分析用户对金融聊天机器人隐私、安全及对话能力的需求，并提炼出可信CUI设计的关键要素。
AI.result: 研究发现，用户在金融场景中更重视隐私和安全保障，而非单纯的对话能力；提出了可信FinBots的设计原则及对自动化财务顾问的实践启示。
AI.conclusion: 本文为金融聊天机器人的可信设计提供了理论框架，证明通过强化隐私与安全机制，FinBots可成为用户日常金融活动的可靠工具。

authors_text: Chinmoy Deka,Abhishek Shrivastava,Ajish K. Abraham,Saurabh Nautiyal,Praveen Chauhan
title: AI-Based Automated Speech Therapy Tools for persons with Speech Sound Disorders: A Systematic Literature Review
title_zh: 针对言语障碍患者的基于人工智能的自动言语治疗工具：系统文献综述
comment: This article has been accepted for publication in Speech, Language and Hearing, published by Taylor & Francis
comments: This article has been accepted for publication in Speech, Language and Hearing, published by Taylor & Francis
abstract: This paper presents a systematic literature review of published studies on AI-based automated speech therapy tools for persons with speech sound disorders (SSD). The COVID-19 pandemic has initiated the requirement for automated speech therapy tools for persons with SSD making speech therapy accessible and affordable. However, there are no guidelines for designing such automated tools and their required degree of automation compared to human experts. In this systematic review, we followed the PRISMA framework to address four research questions: 1) what types of SSD do AI-based automated speech therapy tools address, 2) what is the level of autonomy achieved by such tools, 3) what are the different modes of intervention, and 4) how effective are such tools in comparison with human experts. An extensive search was conducted on digital libraries to find research papers relevant to our study from 2007 to 2022. The results show that AI-based automated speech therapy tools for persons with SSD are increasingly gaining attention among researchers. Articulation disorders were the most frequently addressed SSD based on the reviewed papers. Further, our analysis shows that most researchers proposed fully automated tools without considering the role of other stakeholders. Our review indicates that mobile-based and gamified applications were the most frequent mode of intervention. The results further show that only a few studies compared the effectiveness of such tools compared to expert Speech-Language Pathologists (SLP). Our paper presents the state-of-the-art in the field, contributes significant insights based on the research questions, and provides suggestions for future research directions.
summary: This paper presents a systematic literature review of published studies on AI-based automated speech therapy tools for persons with speech sound disorders (SSD). The COVID-19 pandemic has initiated the requirement for automated speech therapy tools for persons with SSD making speech therapy accessible and affordable. However, there are no guidelines for designing such automated tools and their required degree of automation compared to human experts. In this systematic review, we followed the PRISMA framework to address four research questions: 1) what types of SSD do AI-based automated speech therapy tools address, 2) what is the level of autonomy achieved by such tools, 3) what are the different modes of intervention, and 4) how effective are such tools in comparison with human experts. An extensive search was conducted on digital libraries to find research papers relevant to our study from 2007 to 2022. The results show that AI-based automated speech therapy tools for persons with SSD are increasingly gaining attention among researchers. Articulation disorders were the most frequently addressed SSD based on the reviewed papers. Further, our analysis shows that most researchers proposed fully automated tools without considering the role of other stakeholders. Our review indicates that mobile-based and gamified applications were the most frequent mode of intervention. The results further show that only a few studies compared the effectiveness of such tools compared to expert Speech-Language Pathologists (SLP). Our paper presents the state-of-the-art in the field, contributes significant insights based on the research questions, and provides suggestions for future research directions.
abstract_zh: 本文对针对言语声音障碍（SSD）患者的基于人工智能的自动言语治疗工具的已发表研究进行了系统的文献综述。 COVID-19 大流行引发了对 SSD 患者使用自动化言语治疗工具的需求，使言语治疗变得容易且负担得起。然而，与人类专家相比，没有设计此类自动化工具及其所需自动化程度的指南。在本次系统综述中，我们遵循 PRISMA 框架解决了四个研究问题：1）基于人工智能的自动言语治疗工具可以解决哪些类型的 SSD；2）此类工具实现的自主水平如何；3）有哪些不同的干预模式；4）与人类专家相比，此类工具的有效性如何。我们在数字图书馆中进行了广泛的搜索，以查找 2007 年至 2022 年与我们的研究相关的研究论文。结果表明，针对 SSD 患者的基于人工智能的自动言语治疗工具越来越受到研究人员的关注。根据审阅的论文，发音障碍是最常被解决的 SSD。此外，我们的分析表明，大多数研究人员提出了完全自动化的工具，而没有考虑其他利益相关者的作用。我们的审查表明，基于移动设备和游戏化的应用程序是最常见的干预模式。结果进一步表明，只有少数研究比较了此类工具与语言病理学家 (SLP) 专家的有效性。我们的论文介绍了该领域的最新技术，根据研究问题提供了重要的见解，并为未来的研究方向提供了建议。

AI.tldr: 本文系统回顾了AI驱动的自动化言语治疗工具在言语障碍患者中的应用研究，分析其设计、自主性及效果。
AI.motivation: 新冠疫情推动了自动化言语治疗工具的需求，但目前缺乏设计指南和与人类专家效果对比的研究。
AI.method: 采用PRISMA框架，通过2007至2022年的文献检索，围绕四个研究问题进行系统综述。
AI.result: 多数工具聚焦发音障碍，多为全自动化设计，移动端和游戏化应用为主，仅少数研究对比人类言语治疗师效果。
AI.conclusion: AI工具在该领域受关注度提升，但需进一步研究其有效性及多方利益相关者协作模式。

ai_content.tldr: 本文系统回顾了AI驱动的自动化言语治疗工具在言语障碍患者中的应用研究，分析其设计、自主性及效果。
ai_content.motivation: 新冠疫情推动了自动化言语治疗工具的需求，但目前缺乏设计指南和与人类专家效果对比的研究。

search_text: AI-Based Automated Speech Therapy Tools for persons with Speech Sound Disorders: A Systematic Literature Review 针对言语障碍患者的基于人工智能的自动言语治疗工具：系统文献综述 This paper presents a systematic literature review of published studies on AI-based automated speech therapy tools for persons with speech sound disorders (SSD). The COVID-19 pandemic has initiated the requirement for automated speech therapy tools for persons with SSD making speech therapy accessible and affordable. However, there are no guidelines for designing such automated tools and their required degree of automation compared to human experts. In this systematic review, we followed the PRISMA framework to address four research questions: 1) what types of SSD do AI-based automated speech therapy tools address, 2) what is the level of autonomy achieved by such tools, 3) what are the different modes of intervention, and 4) how effective are such tools in comparison with human experts. An extensive search was conducted on digital libraries to find research papers relevant to our study from 2007 to 2022. The results show that AI-based automated speech therapy tools for persons with SSD are increasingly gaining attention among researchers. Articulation disorders were the most frequently addressed SSD based on the reviewed papers. Further, our analysis shows that most researchers proposed fully automated tools without considering the role of other stakeholders. Our review indicates that mobile-based and gamified applications were the most frequent mode of intervention. The results further show that only a few studies compared the effectiveness of such tools compared to expert Speech-Language Pathologists (SLP). Our paper presents the state-of-the-art in the field, contributes significant insights based on the research questions, and provides suggestions for future research directions. 本文对针对言语声音障碍（SSD）患者的基于人工智能的自动言语治疗工具的已发表研究进行了系统的文献综述。 COVID-19 大流行引发了对 SSD 患者使用自动化言语治疗工具的需求，使言语治疗变得容易且负担得起。然而，与人类专家相比，没有设计此类自动化工具及其所需自动化程度的指南。在本次系统综述中，我们遵循 PRISMA 框架解决了四个研究问题：1）基于人工智能的自动言语治疗工具可以解决哪些类型的 SSD；2）此类工具实现的自主水平如何；3）有哪些不同的干预模式；4）与人类专家相比，此类工具的有效性如何。我们在数字图书馆中进行了广泛的搜索，以查找 2007 年至 2022 年与我们的研究相关的研究论文。结果表明，针对 SSD 患者的基于人工智能的自动言语治疗工具越来越受到研究人员的关注。根据审阅的论文，发音障碍是最常被解决的 SSD。此外，我们的分析表明，大多数研究人员提出了完全自动化的工具，而没有考虑其他利益相关者的作用。我们的审查表明，基于移动设备和游戏化的应用程序是最常见的干预模式。结果进一步表明，只有少数研究比较了此类工具与语言病理学家 (SLP) 专家的有效性。我们的论文介绍了该领域的最新技术，根据研究问题提供了重要的见解，并为未来的研究方向提供了建议。 本文系统回顾了AI驱动的自动化言语治疗工具在言语障碍患者中的应用研究，分析其设计、自主性及效果。 Chinmoy Deka,Abhishek Shrivastava,Ajish K. Abraham,Saurabh Nautiyal,Praveen Chauhan cs.HC,cs.AI

authors_text: Bengt J. Nilsson,Gordana Vujovic
title: Online Two-Dimensional Vector Packing with Advice
title_zh: 带有建议的在线二维向量打包
comment: 15 pages, 4 figures. This an extended version of an article published in "Algorithms and Complexity. CIAC 2021." Lecture Notes in Computer Science, vol 12701. Springer, https://doi.org/10.1007
comments: 15 pages, 4 figures. This an extended version of an article published in "Algorithms and Complexity. CIAC 2021." Lecture Notes in Computer Science, vol 12701. Springer, https://doi.org/10.1007
abstract: We consider the online two-dimensional vector packing problem, showing a lower bound of $11/5$ on the competitive ratio of any {\sc AnyFit} strategy for the problem. We provide strategies with competitive ratio $\max\!\left\{2,6\big/\big(1+3\tan(π/4-γ/2)\big)+ε\right\}$ and logarithmic advice, for any instance where all the input vectors are restricted to have angles in the range $[π/4-γ/2,π/4+γ/2]$, for $0\leqγ<π/3$ and $\max\left\{5/2,4\big/\big(1+2\tan(π/4-γ/2)\big)+ε\right\}$ and logarithmic advice, for any instance where all the input vectors are restricted to have angles in the range $[π/4-γ/2,π/4+γ/2]$, for $0\leqγ\leqπ/3$. In addition, we give a $5/2$-competitive strategy also using logarithmic advice for the unrestricted vectors case. These results should be contrasted to the currently best competitive strategy, FirstFit, having competitive ratio~$27/10$.
summary: We consider the online two-dimensional vector packing problem, showing a lower bound of $11/5$ on the competitive ratio of any {\sc AnyFit} strategy for the problem. We provide strategies with competitive ratio $\max\!\left\{2,6\big/\big(1+3\tan(π/4-γ/2)\big)+ε\right\}$ and logarithmic advice, for any instance where all the input vectors are restricted to have angles in the range $[π/4-γ/2,π/4+γ/2]$, for $0\leqγ<π/3$ and $\max\left\{5/2,4\big/\big(1+2\tan(π/4-γ/2)\big)+ε\right\}$ and logarithmic advice, for any instance where all the input vectors are restricted to have angles in the range $[π/4-γ/2,π/4+γ/2]$, for $0\leqγ\leqπ/3$. In addition, we give a $5/2$-competitive strategy also using logarithmic advice for the unrestricted vectors case. These results should be contrasted to the currently best competitive strategy, FirstFit, having competitive ratio~$27/10$.
abstract_zh: 我们考虑在线二维向量打包问题，显示该问题的任何 {\sc AnyFit} 策略的竞争比的下限为 $11/5$。我们提供具有竞争比 $\max\!\left\{2,6\big/\big(1+3\tan(π/4-γ/2)\big)+ε\right\}$ 和对数建议的策略，适用于所有输入向量的角度限制在 $[π/4-γ/2,π/4+γ/2]$ 范围内的情况，对于 $0\leqγ<π/3$ 和$\max\left\{5/2,4\big/\big(1+2\tan(π/4-γ/2)\big)+ε\right\}$ 和对数建议，对于所有输入向量的角度都限制在 $[π/4-γ/2,π/4+γ/2]$ 范围内的情况，对于 $0\leqγ\leqπ/3$。此外，我们还针对无限制向量情况提供了使用对数建议的 5/2$ 竞争策略。这些结果应与当前最佳竞争策略 FirstFit 进行对比，该策略的竞争比率约为 27/10$。

AI.tldr: 本文研究在线二维向量装箱问题，分析AnyFit策略的下界并提出基于角度限制的改进策略。
AI.motivation: 在线二维向量装箱在资源分配和调度中具有重要应用，现有FirstFit策略的竞争力比为27/10，需探索更优策略及约束条件下的性能提升。
AI.method: 通过分析AnyFit策略的下界，设计基于输入向量角度范围的策略，并结合对数建议（logarithmic advice）优化竞争力比。
AI.result: 证明AnyFit策略下界为11/5，提出角度受限情况下竞争力比为max{2, 6/(1+3tan(π/4-γ/2))+ε}等策略，以及无约束情况下的5/2-competitive策略。
AI.conclusion: 通过角度约束条件和对数建议优化，显著提升了在线二维向量装箱的竞争力比，优于现有FirstFit方法。

authors_text: Adam Tonderski,Joakim Johnander,Christoffer Petersson,Kalle Åström
title: Future Object Detection with Spatiotemporal Transformers
title_zh: 使用时空变换器进行未来目标检测
abstract: We propose the task Future Object Detection, in which the goal is to predict the bounding boxes for all visible objects in a future video frame. While this task involves recognizing temporal and kinematic patterns, in addition to the semantic and geometric ones, it only requires annotations in the standard form for individual, single (future) frames, in contrast to expensive full sequence annotations. We propose to tackle this task with an end-to-end method, in which a detection transformer is trained to directly output the future objects. In order to make accurate predictions about the future, it is necessary to capture the dynamics in the scene, both object motion and the movement of the ego-camera. To this end, we extend existing detection transformers in two ways. First, we experiment with three different mechanisms that enable the network to spatiotemporally process multiple frames. Second, we provide ego-motion information to the model in a learnable manner. We show that both of these extensions improve the future object detection performance substantially. Our final approach learns to capture the dynamics and makes predictions on par with an oracle for prediction horizons up to 100 ms, and outperforms all baselines for longer prediction horizons. By visualizing the attention maps, we observe that a form of tracking emerges within the network. Code is available at github.com/atonderski/future-object-detection.
summary: We propose the task Future Object Detection, in which the goal is to predict the bounding boxes for all visible objects in a future video frame. While this task involves recognizing temporal and kinematic patterns, in addition to the semantic and geometric ones, it only requires annotations in the standard form for individual, single (future) frames, in contrast to expensive full sequence annotations. We propose to tackle this task with an end-to-end method, in which a detection transformer is trained to directly output the future objects. In order to make accurate predictions about the future, it is necessary to capture the dynamics in the scene, both object motion and the movement of the ego-camera. To this end, we extend existing detection transformers in two ways. First, we experiment with three different mechanisms that enable the network to spatiotemporally process multiple frames. Second, we provide ego-motion information to the model in a learnable manner. We show that both of these extensions improve the future object detection performance substantially. Our final approach learns to capture the dynamics and makes predictions on par with an oracle for prediction horizons up to 100 ms, and outperforms all baselines for longer prediction horizons. By visualizing the attention maps, we observe that a form of tracking emerges within the network. Code is available at github.com/atonderski/future-object-detection.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了“未来对象检测”任务，其目标是预测未来视频帧中所有可见对象的边界框。虽然这项任务除了语义和几何模式之外还涉及识别时间和运动模式，但与昂贵的全序列注释相比，它只需要以标准形式对单个（未来）帧进行注释。我们建议使用端到端方法来解决此任务，其中训练检测变压器以直接输出未来对象。为了对未来做出准确的预测，有必要捕捉场景中的动态，包括物体运动和自我相机的运动。为此，我们以两种方式扩展现有的检测变压器。首先，我们尝试了三种不同的机制，使网络能够时空处理多个帧。其次，我们以可学习的方式向模型提供自我运动信息。我们表明，这两个扩展都极大地提高了未来的对象检测性能。我们的最终方法学习捕获动态并在长达 100 毫秒的预测范围内做出与预言机相当的预测，并且在更长的预测范围内优于所有基线。通过可视化注意力图，我们观察到网络中出现了一种跟踪形式。代码可在 github.com/atonderski/future-object-detection 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出未来物体检测任务，利用时空变换器直接预测未来视频帧中所有可见物体的边界框。
AI.motivation: 传统物体检测仅关注当前帧，而未来物体检测需预测未来状态。现有方法依赖昂贵的序列标注，本文提出仅需单帧标注的新任务，降低数据成本并提升预测效率。
AI.method: 设计端到端检测变换器，通过三种时空处理机制分析多帧数据，并以可学习方式引入自运动信息，捕捉场景动态与物体运动规律。
AI.result: 模型在100ms预测范围内表现接近理想情况，长时预测优于所有基线；注意力机制可视化显示网络内部出现跟踪行为。
AI.conclusion: 提出未来物体检测新任务及有效方法，证明时空变换器在捕捉场景动态中的潜力，为自动驾驶等应用提供理论支持。

search_text: Future Object Detection with Spatiotemporal Transformers 使用时空变换器进行未来目标检测 We propose the task Future Object Detection, in which the goal is to predict the bounding boxes for all visible objects in a future video frame. While this task involves recognizing temporal and kinematic patterns, in addition to the semantic and geometric ones, it only requires annotations in the standard form for individual, single (future) frames, in contrast to expensive full sequence annotations. We propose to tackle this task with an end-to-end method, in which a detection transformer is trained to directly output the future objects. In order to make accurate predictions about the future, it is necessary to capture the dynamics in the scene, both object motion and the movement of the ego-camera. To this end, we extend existing detection transformers in two ways. First, we experiment with three different mechanisms that enable the network to spatiotemporally process multiple frames. Second, we provide ego-motion information to the model in a learnable manner. We show that both of these extensions improve the future object detection performance substantially. Our final approach learns to capture the dynamics and makes predictions on par with an oracle for prediction horizons up to 100 ms, and outperforms all baselines for longer prediction horizons. By visualizing the attention maps, we observe that a form of tracking emerges within the network. Code is available at github.com/atonderski/future-object-detection. 我们提出了“未来对象检测”任务，其目标是预测未来视频帧中所有可见对象的边界框。虽然这项任务除了语义和几何模式之外还涉及识别时间和运动模式，但与昂贵的全序列注释相比，它只需要以标准形式对单个（未来）帧进行注释。我们建议使用端到端方法来解决此任务，其中训练检测变压器以直接输出未来对象。为了对未来做出准确的预测，有必要捕捉场景中的动态，包括物体运动和自我相机的运动。为此，我们以两种方式扩展现有的检测变压器。首先，我们尝试了三种不同的机制，使网络能够时空处理多个帧。其次，我们以可学习的方式向模型提供自我运动信息。我们表明，这两个扩展都极大地提高了未来的对象检测性能。我们的最终方法学习捕获动态并在长达 100 毫秒的预测范围内做出与预言机相当的预测，并且在更长的预测范围内优于所有基线。通过可视化注意力图，我们观察到网络中出现了一种跟踪形式。代码可在 github.com/atonderski/future-object-detection 获取。 本文提出未来物体检测任务，利用时空变换器直接预测未来视频帧中所有可见物体的边界框。 Adam Tonderski,Joakim Johnander,Christoffer Petersson,Kalle Åström cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Jimuyang Zhang,Ruizhao Zhu,Eshed Ohn-Bar
title: SelfD: Self-Learning Large-Scale Driving Policies From the Web
title_zh: SelfD：从网络上自学习大规模驾驶策略
abstract: Effectively utilizing the vast amounts of ego-centric navigation data that is freely available on the internet can advance generalized intelligent systems, i.e., to robustly scale across perspectives, platforms, environmental conditions, scenarios, and geographical locations. However, it is difficult to directly leverage such large amounts of unlabeled and highly diverse data for complex 3D reasoning and planning tasks. Consequently, researchers have primarily focused on its use for various auxiliary pixel- and image-level computer vision tasks that do not consider an ultimate navigational objective. In this work, we introduce SelfD, a framework for learning scalable driving by utilizing large amounts of online monocular images. Our key idea is to leverage iterative semi-supervised training when learning imitative agents from unlabeled data. To handle unconstrained viewpoints, scenes, and camera parameters, we train an image-based model that directly learns to plan in the Bird's Eye View (BEV) space. Next, we use unlabeled data to augment the decision-making knowledge and robustness of an initially trained model via self-training. In particular, we propose a pseudo-labeling step which enables making full use of highly diverse demonstration data through "hypothetical" planning-based data augmentation. We employ a large dataset of publicly available YouTube videos to train SelfD and comprehensively analyze its generalization benefits across challenging navigation scenarios. Without requiring any additional data collection or annotation efforts, SelfD demonstrates consistent improvements (by up to 24%) in driving performance evaluation on nuScenes, Argoverse, Waymo, and CARLA.
summary: Effectively utilizing the vast amounts of ego-centric navigation data that is freely available on the internet can advance generalized intelligent systems, i.e., to robustly scale across perspectives, platforms, environmental conditions, scenarios, and geographical locations. However, it is difficult to directly leverage such large amounts of unlabeled and highly diverse data for complex 3D reasoning and planning tasks. Consequently, researchers have primarily focused on its use for various auxiliary pixel- and image-level computer vision tasks that do not consider an ultimate navigational objective. In this work, we introduce SelfD, a framework for learning scalable driving by utilizing large amounts of online monocular images. Our key idea is to leverage iterative semi-supervised training when learning imitative agents from unlabeled data. To handle unconstrained viewpoints, scenes, and camera parameters, we train an image-based model that directly learns to plan in the Bird's Eye View (BEV) space. Next, we use unlabeled data to augment the decision-making knowledge and robustness of an initially trained model via self-training. In particular, we propose a pseudo-labeling step which enables making full use of highly diverse demonstration data through "hypothetical" planning-based data augmentation. We employ a large dataset of publicly available YouTube videos to train SelfD and comprehensively analyze its generalization benefits across challenging navigation scenarios. Without requiring any additional data collection or annotation efforts, SelfD demonstrates consistent improvements (by up to 24%) in driving performance evaluation on nuScenes, Argoverse, Waymo, and CARLA.
abstract_zh: 有效利用互联网上免费提供的大量以自我为中心的导航数据可以推进通用智能系统，即跨视角、平台、环境条件、场景和地理位置进行稳健扩展。然而，直接利用如此大量的未标记且高度多样化的数据来执行复杂的 3D 推理和规划任务是很困难的。因此，研究人员主要关注其在不考虑最终导航目标的各种辅助像素和图像级计算机视觉任务中的使用。在这项工作中，我们介绍了 SelfD，这是一个通过利用大量在线单目图像来学习可扩展驾驶的框架。我们的关键想法是在从未标记的数据中学习模仿代理时利用迭代半监督训练。为了处理不受约束的视点、场景和相机参数，我们训练了一个基于图像的模型，该模型直接学习在鸟瞰（BEV）空间中进行规划。接下来，我们使用未标记的数据通过自我训练来增强初始训练模型的决策知识和鲁棒性。特别是，我们提出了一个伪标记步骤，该步骤可以通过“假设的”基于规划的数据增强来充分利用高度多样化的演示数据。我们使用大量公开的 YouTube 视频数据集来训练 SelfD，并全面分析其在具有挑战性的导航场景中的泛化优势。无需任何额外的数据收集或注释工作，SelfD 在 nuScenes、Argoverse、Waymo 和 CARLA 上的驾驶性能评估方面表现出持续改进（高达 24%）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SelfD框架，通过利用网络上的大量单目图像数据，实现无需标注的自学习大规模驾驶策略。
AI.motivation: 网络上存在大量未标注的视角多样化的导航数据，但直接用于复杂3D规划任务存在困难，现有研究多局限于辅助视觉任务而非导航目标。
AI.method: 采用迭代半监督训练，通过鸟瞰图（BEV）空间建模实现规划，结合伪标签生成与基于假设的规划数据增强，利用YouTube视频数据进行训练。
AI.result: 在nuScenes、Argoverse等数据集上，驾驶性能评估提升最高达24%，且无需额外数据采集或标注。
AI.conclusion: SelfD通过半监督学习与伪标签机制有效利用网络数据，实现了跨场景的驾驶策略泛化，为大规模自学习导航系统提供新思路。

authors_text: Haotian Tang,Zhijian Liu,Xiuyu Li,Yujun Lin,Song Han
title: TorchSparse: Efficient Point Cloud Inference Engine
title_zh: TorchSparse：高效的点云推理引擎
comment: MLSys 2022. The first three authors contributed equally to this work. Project page: https://torchsparse.mit.edu
comments: MLSys 2022. The first three authors contributed equally to this work. Project page: https://torchsparse.mit.edu
abstract: Deep learning on point clouds has received increased attention thanks to its wide applications in AR/VR and autonomous driving. These applications require low latency and high accuracy to provide real-time user experience and ensure user safety. Unlike conventional dense workloads, the sparse and irregular nature of point clouds poses severe challenges to running sparse CNNs efficiently on the general-purpose hardware. Furthermore, existing sparse acceleration techniques for 2D images do not translate to 3D point clouds. In this paper, we introduce TorchSparse, a high-performance point cloud inference engine that accelerates the sparse convolution computation on GPUs. TorchSparse directly optimizes the two bottlenecks of sparse convolution: irregular computation and data movement. It applies adaptive matrix multiplication grouping to trade computation for better regularity, achieving 1.4-1.5x speedup for matrix multiplication. It also optimizes the data movement by adopting vectorized, quantized and fused locality-aware memory access, reducing the memory movement cost by 2.7x. Evaluated on seven representative models across three benchmark datasets, TorchSparse achieves 1.6x and 1.5x measured end-to-end speedup over the state-of-the-art MinkowskiEngine and SpConv, respectively.
summary: Deep learning on point clouds has received increased attention thanks to its wide applications in AR/VR and autonomous driving. These applications require low latency and high accuracy to provide real-time user experience and ensure user safety. Unlike conventional dense workloads, the sparse and irregular nature of point clouds poses severe challenges to running sparse CNNs efficiently on the general-purpose hardware. Furthermore, existing sparse acceleration techniques for 2D images do not translate to 3D point clouds. In this paper, we introduce TorchSparse, a high-performance point cloud inference engine that accelerates the sparse convolution computation on GPUs. TorchSparse directly optimizes the two bottlenecks of sparse convolution: irregular computation and data movement. It applies adaptive matrix multiplication grouping to trade computation for better regularity, achieving 1.4-1.5x speedup for matrix multiplication. It also optimizes the data movement by adopting vectorized, quantized and fused locality-aware memory access, reducing the memory movement cost by 2.7x. Evaluated on seven representative models across three benchmark datasets, TorchSparse achieves 1.6x and 1.5x measured end-to-end speedup over the state-of-the-art MinkowskiEngine and SpConv, respectively.
abstract_zh: 点云深度学习因其在AR/VR和自动驾驶领域的广泛应用而受到越来越多的关注。这些应用需要低延迟和高精度，以提供实时的用户体验并确保用户安全。与传统的密集工作负载不同，点云的稀疏和不规则性质对在通用硬件上有效运行稀疏 CNN 提出了严峻的挑战。此外，现有的 2D 图像稀疏加速技术无法转化为 3D 点云。在本文中，我们介绍了 TorchSparse，这是一种高性能点云推理引擎，可加速 GPU 上的稀疏卷积计算。 TorchSparse直接优化了稀疏卷积的两个瓶颈：不规则计算和数据移动。它应用自适应矩阵乘法分组来交换计算以获得更好的规律性，实现矩阵乘法 1.4-1.5 倍的加速。它还通过采用矢量化、量化和融合局部感知内存访问来优化数据移动，将内存移动成本降低 2.7 倍。在三个基准数据集的七个代表性模型上进行评估后，TorchSparse 的端到端加速比最先进的 MinkowskiEngine 和 SpConv 分别提高了 1.6 倍和 1.5 倍。

AI.tldr: 本文提出TorchSparse，一种高效的点云推理引擎，通过优化稀疏卷积计算提升GPU上的点云处理性能。
AI.motivation: 点云数据的稀疏性和不规则性导致现有2D图像加速技术难以适用于3D场景，而AR/VR和自动驾驶等应用需要低延迟高精度的实时处理。
AI.method: 通过自适应矩阵乘法分组优化计算不规则性，结合向量化、量化及融合局部性内存访问技术减少数据移动开销。
AI.result: 在三个基准数据集的七种模型上，相较MinkowskiEngine和SpConv分别实现1.6x和1.5x的端到端加速，矩阵乘法加速1.4-1.5x，内存移动成本降低2.7x。
AI.conclusion: TorchSparse通过针对性优化稀疏卷积瓶颈，显著提升了点云深度学习的推理效率，为实时应用提供了有效解决方案。

It also optimizes the data movement by adopting vectorized, quantized and fused locality-aware memory access, reducing the memory movement cost by 2.7x. Evaluated on seven representative models across three benchmark datasets, TorchSparse achieves 1.6x and 1.5x measured end-to-end speedup over the state-of-the-art MinkowskiEngine and SpConv, respectively. 点云深度学习因其在AR/VR和自动驾驶领域的广泛应用而受到越来越多的关注。这些应用需要低延迟和高精度，以提供实时的用户体验并确保用户安全。与传统的密集工作负载不同，点云的稀疏和不规则性质对在通用硬件上有效运行稀疏 CNN 提出了严峻的挑战。此外，现有的 2D 图像稀疏加速技术无法转化为 3D 点云。在本文中，我们介绍了 TorchSparse，这是一种高性能点云推理引擎，可加速 GPU 上的稀疏卷积计算。 TorchSparse直接优化了稀疏卷积的两个瓶颈：不规则计算和数据移动。它应用自适应矩阵乘法分组来交换计算以获得更好的规律性，实现矩阵乘法 1.4-1.5 倍的加速。它还通过采用矢量化、量化和融合局部感知内存访问来优化数据移动，将内存移动成本降低 2.7 倍。在三个基准数据集的七个代表性模型上进行评估后，TorchSparse 的端到端加速比最先进的 MinkowskiEngine 和 SpConv 分别提高了 1.6 倍和 1.5 倍。 本文提出TorchSparse，一种高效的点云推理引擎，通过优化稀疏卷积计算提升GPU上的点云处理性能。 Haotian Tang,Zhijian Liu,Xiuyu Li,Yujun Lin,Song Han cs.LG,cs.CV,cs.PF

authors_text: Anthony Garland,Kevin Potter,Matt Smith
title: Feature anomaly detection system (FADS) for intelligent manufacturing
title_zh: 智能制造特征异常检测系统（FADS）
abstract: Anomaly detection is important for industrial automation and part quality assurance, and while humans can easily detect anomalies in components given a few examples, designing a generic automated system that can perform at human or above human capabilities remains a challenge. In this work, we present a simple new anomaly detection algorithm called FADS (feature-based anomaly detection system) which leverages pretrained convolutional neural networks (CNN) to generate a statistical model of nominal inputs by observing the activation of the convolutional filters. During inference the system compares the convolutional filter activation of the new input to the statistical model and flags activations that are outside the expected range of values and therefore likely an anomaly. By using a pretrained network, FADS demonstrates excellent performance similar to or better than other machine learning approaches to anomaly detection while at the same time FADS requires no tuning of the CNN weights. We demonstrate FADS ability by detecting process parameter changes on a custom dataset of additively manufactured lattices. The FADS localization algorithm shows that textural differences that are visible on the surface can be used to detect process parameter changes. In addition, we test FADS on benchmark datasets, such as the MVTec Anomaly Detection dataset, and report good results.
summary: Anomaly detection is important for industrial automation and part quality assurance, and while humans can easily detect anomalies in components given a few examples, designing a generic automated system that can perform at human or above human capabilities remains a challenge. In this work, we present a simple new anomaly detection algorithm called FADS (feature-based anomaly detection system) which leverages pretrained convolutional neural networks (CNN) to generate a statistical model of nominal inputs by observing the activation of the convolutional filters. During inference the system compares the convolutional filter activation of the new input to the statistical model and flags activations that are outside the expected range of values and therefore likely an anomaly. By using a pretrained network, FADS demonstrates excellent performance similar to or better than other machine learning approaches to anomaly detection while at the same time FADS requires no tuning of the CNN weights. We demonstrate FADS ability by detecting process parameter changes on a custom dataset of additively manufactured lattices. The FADS localization algorithm shows that textural differences that are visible on the surface can be used to detect process parameter changes. In addition, we test FADS on benchmark datasets, such as the MVTec Anomaly Detection dataset, and report good results.
abstract_zh: 异常检测对于工业自动化和零件质量保证非常重要，虽然人类可以轻松地检测到组件中的异常（举几个例子），但设计一个能够以人类或超越人类能力执行的通用自动化系统仍然是一个挑战。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种简单的新异常检测算法，称为 FADS（基于特征的异常检测系统），该算法利用预训练的卷积神经网络（CNN）通过观察卷积滤波器的激活来生成标称输入的统计模型。在推理过程中，系统会将新输入的卷积滤波器激活与统计模型进行比较，并标记超出预期值范围的激活，因此可能存在异常。通过使用预训练网络，FADS 表现出了与其他机器学习方法类似或更好的异常检测性能，同时 FADS 不需要调整 CNN 权重。我们通过检测增材制造晶格的自定义数据集上的工艺参数变化来展示 FADS 能力。 FADS 定位算法表明，表面可见的纹理差异可用于检测过程参数的变化。此外，我们还在基准数据集（例如 MVTec 异常检测数据集）上测试了 FADS，并报告了良好的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于特征的异常检测系统FADS，用于智能制造中的异常检测。
AI.motivation: 尽管人类能通过少量示例检测组件异常，但设计出具有人类水平或超越人类能力的通用自动化检测系统仍是挑战。
AI.method: 利用预训练卷积神经网络（CNN）生成正常输入的统计模型，通过比较新输入的卷积滤波器激活值与统计模型来检测异常。
AI.result: FADS在自定义增材制造晶格数据集和MVTec基准数据集上均表现出优异性能，且无需调整CNN权重。
AI.conclusion: FADS通过纹理差异定位算法有效检测工艺参数变化，为智能制造提供了无需调参的高效异常检测方案。

authors_text: Muntasir Wahed,Afrina Tabassum,Ismini Lourentzou
title: Adversarial Contrastive Learning by Permuting Cluster Assignments
title_zh: 通过排列簇分配进行对抗性对比学习
abstract: Contrastive learning has gained popularity as an effective self-supervised representation learning technique. Several research directions improve traditional contrastive approaches, e.g., prototypical contrastive methods better capture the semantic similarity among instances and reduce the computational burden by considering cluster prototypes or cluster assignments, while adversarial instance-wise contrastive methods improve robustness against a variety of attacks. To the best of our knowledge, no prior work jointly considers robustness, cluster-wise semantic similarity and computational efficiency. In this work, we propose SwARo, an adversarial contrastive framework that incorporates cluster assignment permutations to generate representative adversarial samples. We evaluate SwARo on multiple benchmark datasets and against various white-box and black-box attacks, obtaining consistent improvements over state-of-the-art baselines.
summary: Contrastive learning has gained popularity as an effective self-supervised representation learning technique. Several research directions improve traditional contrastive approaches, e.g., prototypical contrastive methods better capture the semantic similarity among instances and reduce the computational burden by considering cluster prototypes or cluster assignments, while adversarial instance-wise contrastive methods improve robustness against a variety of attacks. To the best of our knowledge, no prior work jointly considers robustness, cluster-wise semantic similarity and computational efficiency. In this work, we propose SwARo, an adversarial contrastive framework that incorporates cluster assignment permutations to generate representative adversarial samples. We evaluate SwARo on multiple benchmark datasets and against various white-box and black-box attacks, obtaining consistent improvements over state-of-the-art baselines.
abstract_zh: 对比学习作为一种有效的自监督表征学习技术已经受到欢迎。几个研究方向改进了传统的对比方法，例如，原型对比方法可以更好地捕获实例之间的语义相似性，并通过考虑集群原型或集群分配来减少计算负担，而对抗性实例对比方法可以提高针对各种攻击的鲁棒性。据我们所知，之前的工作还没有共同考虑鲁棒性、集群语义相似性和计算效率。在这项工作中，我们提出了 SwARo，一种对抗性对比框架，它结合了聚类分配排列来生成代表性的对抗性样本。我们在多个基准数据集上评估 SwARo，并针对各种白盒和黑盒攻击，获得相对于最先进基线的一致改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SwARo框架，通过置换聚类分配生成对抗样本，结合对抗对比学习与聚类原型方法，提升模型鲁棒性与计算效率。
AI.motivation: 现有对比学习方法或侧重聚类语义相似性，或增强对抗鲁棒性，但缺乏同时兼顾三者（鲁棒性、语义相似性、计算效率）的统一框架。
AI.method: 设计SwARo框架，利用聚类分配置换生成对抗样本，在对比学习中引入对抗扰动，同时保持原型聚类的计算优势。
AI.result: 在多个基准数据集上，SwARo在白盒和黑盒攻击下均优于现有最先进方法，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: SwARo通过联合优化对抗鲁棒性与聚类语义，为高效且安全的自监督表征学习提供了新方向。

authors_text: Giancarlo Paoletti,Jacopo Cavazza,Cigdem Beyan,Alessio Del Bue
title: Unsupervised Human Action Recognition with Skeletal Graph Laplacian and Self-Supervised Viewpoints Invariance
title_zh: 具有骨骼图拉普拉斯和自监督视点不变性的无监督人类行为识别
abstract: This paper presents a novel end-to-end method for the problem of skeleton-based unsupervised human action recognition. We propose a new architecture with a convolutional autoencoder that uses graph Laplacian regularization to model the skeletal geometry across the temporal dynamics of actions. Our approach is robust towards viewpoint variations by including a self-supervised gradient reverse layer that ensures generalization across camera views. The proposed method is validated on NTU-60 and NTU-120 large-scale datasets in which it outperforms all prior unsupervised skeleton-based approaches on the cross-subject, cross-view, and cross-setup protocols. Although unsupervised, our learnable representation allows our method even to surpass a few supervised skeleton-based action recognition methods. The code is available in: www.github.com/IIT-PAVIS/UHAR_Skeletal_Laplacian
summary: This paper presents a novel end-to-end method for the problem of skeleton-based unsupervised human action recognition. We propose a new architecture with a convolutional autoencoder that uses graph Laplacian regularization to model the skeletal geometry across the temporal dynamics of actions. Our approach is robust towards viewpoint variations by including a self-supervised gradient reverse layer that ensures generalization across camera views. The proposed method is validated on NTU-60 and NTU-120 large-scale datasets in which it outperforms all prior unsupervised skeleton-based approaches on the cross-subject, cross-view, and cross-setup protocols. Although unsupervised, our learnable representation allows our method even to surpass a few supervised skeleton-based action recognition methods. The code is available in: www.github.com/IIT-PAVIS/UHAR_Skeletal_Laplacian
abstract_zh: 本文针对基于骨架的无监督人体动作识别问题提出了一种新颖的端到端方法。我们提出了一种带有卷积自动编码器的新架构，该架构使用图拉普拉斯正则化来对动作的时间动态中的骨骼几何进行建模。我们的方法通过包含一个自我监督的梯度反向层来确保跨摄像机视图的泛化，从而对视点变化具有鲁棒性。所提出的方法在 NTU-60 和 NTU-120 大规模数据集上进行了验证，在跨主题、跨视图和跨设置协议上，它优于所有先前的无监督基于骨架的方法。尽管不受监督，但我们的可学习表示使我们的方法甚至超越了一些基于监督骨架的动作识别方法。代码位于：www.github.com/IIT-PAVIS/UHAR_Skeletal_Laplacian

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于骨骼的无监督人类动作识别方法，通过图拉普拉斯正则化和自监督视角不变性技术建模动作时空特征。
AI.motivation: 现有骨骼动作识别方法依赖标注数据且难以应对视角变化，需开发无需监督且视角鲁棒的解决方案。
AI.method: 设计包含图拉普拉斯正则化的卷积自编码器架构，并引入自监督梯度反转层实现跨视角泛化。
AI.result: 在NTU-60/120数据集上，该方法在跨主体、跨视角和跨设置协议中超越所有无监督方法，甚至优于部分有监督方法。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过几何建模与视角不变性学习，有效解决了无监督骨骼动作识别中的关键挑战，具有实际应用价值。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于骨骼的无监督人类动作识别方法，通过图拉普拉斯正则化和自监督视角不变性技术建模动作时空特征。
ai_content.motivation: 现有骨骼动作识别方法依赖标注数据且难以应对视角变化，需开发无需监督且视角鲁棒的解决方案。
ai_content.method: 设计包含图拉普拉斯正则化的卷积自编码器架构，并引入自监督梯度反转层实现跨视角泛化。
ai_content.result: 在NTU-60/120数据集上，该方法在跨主体、跨视角和跨设置协议中超越所有无监督方法，甚至优于部分有监督方法。
ai_content.conclusion: 所提方法通过几何建模与视角不变性学习，有效解决了无监督骨骼动作识别中的关键挑战，具有实际应用价值。
